title: The Hart Notice
tagline: A clipped notice from 1912 refuses to stay ordinary.

opening.beat_intro: A torn column from the Evening Star lands on your board. The ink is uneven; someone wanted this seen.

fragment.raw_ocr: GEORGETOWN — M. Hart will receive inquiries at the room above the stationers, 14th St., after 8 p.m. Bring the phrase. No names at the desk.
fragment.clean_text: Georgetown — M. Hart will receive inquiries at the room above the stationers on 14th Street after 8 p.m. Bring the phrase. No names at the desk.

evidence_cards.title: Bring the phrase
evidence_cards.detail: The notice demands a passphrase, not a name — unusual for a public advertisement.

evidence_cards.title: No names at the desk
evidence_cards.detail: Whoever placed this expects anonymity from the shop staff.

evidence_cards.title: After 8 p.m. only
evidence_cards.detail: Late-hour meetings suggest discretion or something to hide from daylight trade.

direct_archive_facts: Quoted lines match the OCR fragment verbatim
Georgetown and 14th St. appear in the clipping

person.beat_intro: City directories and society pages disagree on the initial — but not on the reputation.

fragment.raw_ocr: M. Hart — lately of the canal office — has been seen conferring with counsel. Initial uncertain in directory.
fragment.clean_text: M. Hart — lately of the canal office — has been seen conferring with counsel. The city directory lists the initial as uncertain.

evidence_cards.title: Canal office tie
evidence_cards.detail: Hart's recent post suggests access to routing records or liens — leverage, not gossip.

evidence_cards.title: Uncertain initial
evidence_cards.detail: Directories usually nail a middle initial; here it is deliberately vague.

direct_archive_facts: Canal office reference appears in a related society brief
Directory uncertainty is quoted from the fragment
reveal_notes.bridge_notes: Connecting Hart to the original notice is editorial glue; the notice text itself is unchanged from beat one.

place.beat_intro: Georgetown in March is mud, gaslight, and whispers carried faster than the streetcars.

fragment.raw_ocr: GEORGETOWN — The stationers' row sees heavy foot traffic from clerks leaving the canal hearings. Rooms above shops let by the week.
fragment.clean_text: Georgetown — The stationers' row sees heavy foot traffic from clerks leaving the canal hearings. Rooms above the shops are let by the week.

evidence_cards.title: Canal hearings nearby
evidence_cards.detail: The same week as the notice, Georgetown clerks shuttle between shops and testimony.

evidence_cards.title: Room above the shop
evidence_cards.detail: Weekly lets explain why Hart can use a stationer's address without working the counter.

direct_archive_facts: Stationers' row and canal hearings language from the fragment
Date within 48 hours of the original notice
reveal_notes.bridge_notes: Geographic context is archival; the causal link to Hart's meeting is inferred for play.

phrase.beat_intro: Two words in the notice repeat nowhere else on the page — except the margins.

fragment.raw_ocr: Margin note (illegible) — clerk copies: 'Bring the phrase' matches ledger shorthand for sealed routing.
fragment.clean_text: Margin note — clerk copies: 'Bring the phrase' matches ledger shorthand for sealed routing instructions.

evidence_cards.title: Ledger shorthand
evidence_cards.detail: Canal clerks used compressed phrases for packages that should not be named in open ads.

evidence_cards.title: Margin echo
evidence_cards.detail: The same phrase appears in the margin of an unrelated shipping brief — copyist error or signal?

direct_archive_facts: 'Bring the phrase' appears verbatim in the primary notice
Shorthand explanation paraphrases a clerks' glossary also on Chronicling America
reveal_notes.bridge_notes: Margin-note beat is a compressed reading of multiple tiny fragments for demo pacing.

closed.beat_intro: You pin the board shut. Enough truth for one night — the archive keeps the rest.

fragment.clean_text: Case tabled. Three leads examined. Archive fragments logged.

evidence_cards.title: What you proved
evidence_cards.detail: The notice was real, time-bound, and designed for anonymous contact — not a prank classified.

direct_archive_facts: All quoted notice language originated from beat one OCR
Supporting beats used paraphrased public-domain adjacent snippets for demo
reveal_notes.bridge_notes: Demo case — swap in fully sourced clue packs before submission.
